concerns.text: Task T017 ('Integrate all collectors... and SUS questionnaire') is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T015 (Counterbalancer) and T016 (Metrics Collector) which are also marked [P]. Integration tasks cannot be parallel-safe if they consume artifacts from other parallel tasks; T017 must be ordered AFTER T015 and T016 complete, not parallel with them.

Task T019 (Raw data logging) is marked as unchecked `[ ]` (incomplete) but is listed as a hard dependency for Phase 5 (T021, T023). The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section states 'Phase 5 cannot start until Phase 4 (T019) completes'. A consumer task (T021) cannot be scheduled in a parallel/next phase if its producer (T019) is not marked complete or guaranteed to run first. This creates a semantic ordering violation where the analysis phase is blocked by an unimplemented prerequisite.

Task T021 (Data cleaning) depends on T019 (Raw data logging). T019 is currently unchecked `[ ]`. T021 is marked `[P]` (parallel-safe). T021 cannot be parallel-safe if it requires the output of T019. The [P] tag is incorrect here; T021 must be strictly sequential after T019.

Task T027 (Visualization) depends on T026 (Metrics Summary). T026 is marked `[X]` (complete) in the text, but T025 (Orchestration script) is marked `[ ]` (incomplete). T026 logically requires T025 to run the analysis pipeline. If T025 is not complete, T026 cannot be complete. The ordering implies T027 can proceed, but the artifact flow is broken because the producer (T025) is missing.
T017 description mentions 'requires T016, T015, T012a' but is tagged `[P]`. The [P] tag contradicts the explicit dependency list in the description. Remove [P] or reorder the task to be sequential.
